Name: Women in HPC (WHPC)
Description: International community supporting women and underrepresented groups in HPC through mentoring, networking, leadership development, conferences, and career opportunities.

Name: Campus Research Computing Consortium (CaRCC)
Description: Coalition advancing research computing and data careers through professionalization, workforce development, and community building. Career tracks, mentoring, and working groups.

Name: SGCI / SGX3 (Science Gateways Community Institute)
Category: Communities & Mentorship, Training Programs, Cyberinfrastructure Resources
Description: National organization that provides training, consulting, software, and community support for developing and sustaining Science Gateways.

Name: ACCESS Campus Champions
Description: National network of research computing advocates helping researchers and students access cyberinfrastructure resources, training, and technical expertise from ACCESS (Advanced Cyberinfrastructure Coordination Ecosystem: Services & Support).

Name: Open HPC
Description: Industry consortium driving HPC infrastructure innovation through open-source collaboration. Provides standardized HPC distributions, interoperability specifications, and a community platform for vendors, researchers, and users to advance HPC systems and software.

Name: Black in AI
Description: Global community broadening Black representation in AI through conferences, workshops, mentorship, and career development.

Name: MS-CC (Minority Serving Institutions Cyberinfrastructure Consortium)
Description: Consortium supporting cyberinfrastructure capacity building at Minority Serving Institutions. Develops CS/IT programs and research infrastructure at HBCUs, MSIs, community colleges.

Name: PathwaysToScience.org
Description: Comprehensive hub connecting Indigenous, Black, Hispanic/Latinx, and LGBTQ+ students and professionals with STEM career resources, fellowships, conferences, and funding.

Name: NSBE (National Society of Black Engineers)
Description: The largest and most visible member-serving organization committed to the success and academic achievement of Black engineers. Offers mentorship, professional development, career fairs, and HPC/scholarship programs.

Name: SACNAS (Society for Advancement of Chicanos/Hispanics and Native Americans in Science)
Description: Leading STEM organization advancing Hispanic and Native American scientists. Provides mentorship, professional development, networking events, and scholarships at all career stages.

Name: Latinas in STEM
Description: Advancing Latinas in scientific and technical careers through mentoring, advocacy, scholarships, and professional development.

Name: SHPE (Society of Hispanic Professional Engineers)
Description: The world's largest resource for empowering the Latinx community to achieve their goals as engineers and scientists. Offers conferences, scholarships, career fairs, and professional development.

Name: Association for Computing Machinery (ACM)
Description: Association for Computing Machinery - the world's largest educational and scientific computing society. Student memberships, SIGs, and conferences.

Name: IEEE Computer Society
Description: IEEE (Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers) Computer Society advancing computing theory and practice. Student memberships and specialized technical committees.

Name: US-RSE (Research Software Engineering Association)
Description: Professional organization for Scientific Computing and Research Software Engineering. Offers networking, professional development, job opportunities, and community building. On-site exhibitor at PEARC26.

Name: ACCESS Careers
Description: Career board from ACCESS (Advanced Cyberinfrastructure Coordination Ecosystem: Services & Support) listing computing and cyberinfrastructure positions at academic and research institutions nationwide.

Name: CaRCC HR Job Family Matrix
Description: Framework for research computing and data career paths, including salary benchmarks and job family definitions.

Name: NRP (National Research Platform)
Description: Provides accessible cloud-based computational resources, data science tools, and computing infrastructure for researchers and students across all disciplines.

Name: Jupyter
Description: Interactive notebook platform supporting Python, Julia, R, and other languages for data science and education.

Name: Apptainer
Description: Open-source container platform designed for HPC and scientific computing. Successor to Singularity, enabling reproducible, portable computing environments for research workflows.

Name: ACCESS Cyberinfrastructure
Description: NSF-funded ACCESS (Advanced Cyberinfrastructure Coordination Ecosystem: Services & Support) program providing researchers and educators access to national supercomputers, storage, GPUs, and training. On-site exhibitor at PEARC26.

Name: NSF GRFP
Description: National Science Foundation Graduate Research Fellowship Program for STEM students pursuing research-based careers.

Name: DOE CSGF
Description: Department of Energy Computational Science Graduate Fellowship for Ph.D. students using high-performance computing in science and engineering research. Provides a $45,000 annual stipend, full tuition, and a research practicum at a DOE laboratory.

Name: ACCESS Workshops
Description: Workshops and training events from ACCESS (Advanced Cyberinfrastructure Coordination Ecosystem: Services & Support) covering HPC, data science, visualization, and cyberinfrastructure tools.

Name: NCSA Learn
Description: NCSA's free online learning portal featuring self-paced courses on HPC, Linux, Python, Git, CUDA, and research computing.

Name: BSSW - Formal Course Resources for Learning About HPC
Description: Argonne/Sandia-curated article compiling university courses and educational materials on high-performance computing.

Name: Intro to HPC - SLURM Training (Cambi)
Description: Free online self-study module covering SLURM workload manager basics - job submission, resource requests, batch scripting, squeue/sinfo/sbatch commands, and job arrays.

Name: HSF Training - Singularity Container Training
Description: High Energy Physics community's training material on Singularity containers for HPC. Covers container basics, workflows, data management, and security.

Name: The Carpentries - Adopting HPC Carpentry
Description: Carpentries blog post on strategies, lessons learned, and resources for institutions adopting HPC Carpentry workshops.

Name: marimo
Description: Programmable notebook environment with reactive, live-updating notebooks for data exploration and collaboration. Improved reproducibility over traditional formats.

Name: Google AI Professional Certificate
Description: Google's online professional certificate covering foundational AI and ML concepts - prompt engineering, LLMs, and practical AI applications.

Name: TACC Undergraduate & Graduate Education
Description: Texas Advanced Computing Center's education programs for undergraduate and graduate students. Includes REU programs, training workshops, graduate fellowships, and hands-on research experiences.

Name: SC (Supercomputing Conference)
Description: Premier international conference for HPC, data analytics, and supercomputing. Largest gathering of HPC professionals worldwide with technical sessions, exhibitions, and networking.

Name: PEARC (Practical and Extreme Computing Conference)
Description: Flagship conference for research computing professionals and students. Offers tutorials, short courses, and student travel support.

Name: HPDC (High-Performance Parallel and Distributed Computing)
Description: ACM International Symposium on High-Performance Parallel and Distributed Computing, the premier venue for research on clusters, grids, clouds, and multicore computing systems.

Name: IEEE IPDPS (International Parallel and Distributed Processing Symposium)
Description: IEEE Computer Society's premier symposium on parallel and distributed processing, featuring technical sessions, workshops, tutorials, and exhibits.

Name: SIAM CSI (Computing in Science and Engineering)
Description: SIAM conference showcasing computational research across engineering, science, and mathematics. Bridges theory and practice in computational methods.

Name: SIAM PP (Parallel Processing for Scientific Computing)
Description: SIAM conference focused on parallel computing for scientific computing. Brings together researchers developing parallel algorithms and applications.

Name: IEEE Cluster Computing
Description: IEEE (Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers) International Conference on Cluster Computing. Covers cluster computing architectures, middleware, algorithms, and applications for scientific and commercial use.

Name: ISC High Performance
Description: Formerly the International Supercomputing Conference (ISC) before its 2015 rebrand; international conference and exhibition for high-performance computing, data analytics, and networking. Major European HPC conference with global participation.

Name: HPCWire
Description: Leading publication covering the high-performance computing industry, news, insights, and thought leadership.

Name: NSF REU Sites
Description: NSF Research Experiences for Undergraduates program listing undergraduate research internships at US institutions.

Name: NSF REU Search Tool
Description: Advanced search tool to find NSF-funded REU (Research Experiences for Undergraduates) sites beyond the main listing page. Filter by state, institution type, discipline, and funding source.

Name: Google Summer of Code (GSoC)
Description: Google's program for undergraduate students worldwide to contribute to open source development with mentorship.

Name: NASA SEED
Description: NASA Space Engineering Research and Design program for undergraduate and graduate students on real NASA missions.

Name: Anvil Supercomputer
Description: Purdue Research Computing Access (RCAC)'s high-performance computing facility. Features peta-scale computing, data storage, and visualization resources for research across all disciplines.

Name: PATh/Open Science Pool
Description: Open Science Pool provides a massive, shared pool of computing resources for research and education. PATh (The Partnership to Advance Throughput Computing) connects researchers to these distributed computing resources across academic and cloud environments.

Name: SULI Program (Science Undergraduate Laboratory Internships)
Description: A Department of Energy (DOE) program that offers hands-on research internship opportunities for undergraduates at national laboratories.

Name: Open Quantum Initiative (OQI) Undergraduate Fellowship
Description: A 10-week, fully funded summer research opportunity placing students at quantum research sites across the Midwest, including a $7,000 stipend and travel coverage.

Name: Q-Ready Program
Description: An online program that helps students and postdocs explore quantum careers, prepare for career paths, and meet scientists hiring for these roles.

Name: Chicago Quantum Exchange Job Board
Description: A dedicated job board to browse and sign up for alerts regarding entry-level jobs and internships in quantum computing, sensing, and networks.

Name: Quantum Education Landscape (RIT)
Description: An overview of programs, courses, minors, and majors focused on quantum information science and engineering at various institutions.

Name: Quantum Learning Resources
Description: Do-it-yourself educational resources to practice and learn quantum coding using kits like IBM's Qiskit and Xanadu's Pennylane.

Name: SGX3 Fall & Spring Training
Description: Upcoming advanced computational training sessions (Fall runs Oct-Dec, Wednesday nights) offering a $500 stipend to students, continuing the education from the summer Coding Institute.

Name: TACC Analysis Portal (TAP)
Description: Web-based interface from TACC (Texas Advanced Computing Center) for launching HPC applications and workflows directly from a browser without command-line experience.

Name: NAIRR Pilot Portal
Description: The National Artificial Intelligence Research Resource pilot portal managed by SGX3 for the NSF to share computing and AI resources across the research community

Name: NCAR Internships
Description: High-performance computing and data science internship opportunities at the National Center for Atmospheric Research (NCAR)

Name: ACCESS Allocations
Description: Portals for requesting open-source computational resources provided by ACCESS (Advanced Cyberinfrastructure Coordination Ecosystem: Services & Support), an NSF-funded program.

Name: Cloudbank ACCESS
Description: A computational resource allocation service provided by the ACCESS (Advanced Cyberinfrastructure Coordination Ecosystem: Services & Support) program.

Name: Benjamin Gilman Scholarship
Description: A scholarship program (provided by the U.S. Department of State) providing funding for students engaging in study abroad opportunities

Name: NCAR Explorer Series
Description: Public lectures and educational outreach materials published by NCAR (National Center for Atmospheric Research).

Name: NCAR PI-WRF Teaching Box
Description: Educational resources and teaching boxes provided by NCAR (National Center for Atmospheric Research) for weather modeling.

Name: WeTeachCS
Description: An educational program run by TACC designed to prepare K-12 teachers for computer science certifications

Name: Cambridge Computer
Description: Helps universities, labs, and research-driven enterprises design and deploy research computing, AI, HPC, storage, networking, and on-prem cloud infrastructure. Title sponsor since 1991; NVIDIA and VAST Data award winner.

Name: Amazon Web Services (AWS)
Description: World's most comprehensive, broadly adopted cloud platform, offering extensive cloud and AI capabilities on global infrastructure.

Name: DataDirect Networks (DDN)
Description: Data Intelligence Platform built for AI practitioners; partners with NVIDIA, Supermicro, and Google Cloud on large-scale AI training and inference infrastructure.

Name: Google Cloud
Description: Full-stack, AI-optimized ecosystem for academia with 150+ products, 200+ foundation models, and 100PB+ of open datasets.

Name: CIQ
Description: Founding support/services partner of Rocky Linux; delivers enterprise Linux infrastructure for sovereign AI and HPC (Rocky Linux, Ascender Pro, Fuzzball, Warewulf Pro, Apptainer).

Name: Cirrascale
Description: "The Expert Neocloud for Private AI."

Name: Dell Technologies
Description: Broad technology and services portfolio for the AI era across devices, infrastructure, and services.

Name: HPE (with AMD)
Description: Enterprise technology leader combining AI, cloud, and networking to optimize performance and turn data into foresight.

Name: Lenovo (with AMD)
Description: $69B global tech company; full-stack AI-enabled/ready/optimized devices, infrastructure, software and services.

Name: Internet2
Description: Nonprofit tech community providing high-speed national network, eduroam, cloud, and IAM (InCommon) services for research/education.

Name: SHI Public Sector (with Dell)
Description: Dedicated R1 team providing tailored IT procurement, security, and innovation support for research institutions.

Name: AuriStor
Description: Global Namespace Distributed File System for multi-site, geographically distributed storage with strong security and scalability.

Name: Mark III Systems
Description: NVIDIA Elite Partner building AI, GenAI, Agentic AI, HPC, and digital twin Centers of Excellence with research institutions.

Name: National Center for Supercomputing Applications (NCSA)
Description: Since 1986, a hub for transdisciplinary research and advanced cyberinfrastructure at the University of Illinois.

Name: Atempo
Description: Miria platform for moving, protecting, archiving, and migrating unstructured data across file, object, tape, and cloud storage.

Name: BeeGFS (ThinkParQ)
Description: Leading parallel file system for HPC, AI/ML, media, life sciences, and oil & gas; Community and Enterprise editions.

Name: Coalition for Academic Scientific Computation (CASC)
Description: National nonprofit representing academic HPC/research computing institutions; advocacy and policy engagement with federal agencies.

Name: ConfidentialMind
Description: Platform for deploying generative AI apps/agents on confidential data within your own environment. Based in Espoo, Finland & San Francisco.

Name: Cornelis Networks
Description: Open, intelligent high-performance networking solutions for demanding technical computing.

Name: Globus
Description: Data management and compute platform (Univ. of Chicago) for secure file transfer, sharing, and automation; connects 50,000+ orgs in 80+ countries.

Name: Microway
Description: Since 1982, designs HPC/AI hardware — clusters, servers, workstations, storage, and fabric solutions.

Name: Neuvys Technologies / NetApp
Description: Neuvys: woman-owned HPC/AI systems integrator. NetApp: Intelligent Data Infrastructure company built on ONTAP, AI Data Engine, and AFX.

Name: NICS / AI Tennessee
Description: NICS (Univ. of Tennessee): HPC research, AI workload optimization. AI Tennessee: statewide initiative advancing AI readiness and workforce development.

Name: Omnibond
Description: projectEureka: hybrid platform for persistent, browser-accessible AI/simulation workspaces; also maintains OrangeFS parallel file system.

Name: Open OnDemand
Description: Provides remote web access to supercomputers for students, researchers, and professionals.

Name: Pacific Northwest National Laboratory (PNNL)
Description: National lab with strengths in chemistry, Earth sciences, biology, and data science; Research Computing division offers HPC/GPU access and consulting.

Name: PIER Group
Description: Formed 2018; delivers campus infrastructure, HPC, optical networking, and procurement solutions for research & education.

Name: PSC (Pittsburgh Supercomputing Center)
Description: Joint center (Carnegie Mellon + Univ. of Pittsburgh), established 1986; part of NSF cyberinfrastructure program.

Name: Purdue University (RCAC)
Description: Rosen Center for Advanced Computing: HPC, data storage, and research software engineering services; industry workforce training.

Name: Qblox
Description: Global leader in quantum control stacks — modular hardware/software for qubit manipulation; backed by €26M Series A.

Name: San Diego Supercomputer Center (SDSC)
Description: Premier HPC/data-intensive computing center since the 1980s; booth features SGX3, CloudBank, and systems like Expanse and Voyager.

Name: Sustainable Horizons Institute
Description: Builds inclusive communities in science/tech; supports students, faculty, and professionals in scientific computing.

Name: Abundant Frontier Institute (AFI)
Description: Nonprofit bridging the technology gap through hands-on experiential learning — empowering educators at under-resourced institutions to teach AI and emerging technologies, and helping organizations build internal AI capability through project-based training.

Name: AI Credits Directory
Description: Community-maintained index of free AI API credits, GPU grants, and cloud programs, including instant-signup providers and student-specific offers.

Name: AI Perks Free API Credits Guide (2026)
Description: Step-by-step guide to aggregating over $10,000 in free AI API credits from OpenAI, Anthropic, Mistral, Cerebras, Groq, and more.

Name: AI Squared Student Program (UNIFI)
Category: AI Tools & Resources, Training Programs, Research Opportunities
Description: Free access to AI Squared's UNIFI platform for students, for building, deploying, and integrating AI applications with existing business systems.

Name: Anthropic
Description: AI safety company creating Claude and other language models.

Name: Cerebras Cloud (Inference API)
Description: Ultra-fast LLM inference on Wafer-Scale Engine hardware, with 1,000,000 free tokens per day, permanently. No .edu email, credit card, or waitlist required.

Name: Exa AI (Neural Search API)
Description: Neural search and web crawling API for AI agents and research workflows, with structured search results and full-page content extraction. $10 in free credits on student signup.

Name: Free AI Tools GitHub List
Description: Community-maintained, curated list of free and low-cost AI tools, LLM APIs, IDEs, agent frameworks, and infrastructure options.

Name: Free.ai Education
Description: Unified AI platform giving students Pro-tier access to hundreds of AI tools and models, including GPT-4o, Claude, and Gemini. Includes 500,000 tokens/month and full API access; no credit card required. Requires a .edu email to sign up, re-verified monthly.

Name: GitHub Student Developer Pack
Description: Bundle of 100+ professional developer tools, AI coding assistants, and cloud credits, including GitHub Copilot Pro (with Claude 3.5 Sonnet and GPT-4.1 access), Azure and DigitalOcean credits, and JetBrains IDEs. Requires .edu email or school ID verification, 1-3 business day approval.

Name: Google AI Studio (Gemini API)
Description: Google's developer environment for the Gemini model family, with a generous free API tier. No .edu email or credit card required — sign in with any Google account for an instant API key.

Name: Google Gemini
Description: Google's advanced AI assistant for creative work.

Name: Groq Cloud
Description: High-speed LLM inference on Groq LPU hardware, with roughly 500K-1M free tokens/day across Llama, Qwen, and DeepSeek models. No .edu email or credit card required.

Name: Hugging Face
Description: Open-source platform for machine learning models and datasets.

Name: LM Studio
Description: Desktop app for discovering, downloading, and running local large language models with a graphical interface, no coding required.

Name: Microsoft Copilot
Description: AI assistant powered by OpenAI technology.

Name: Ollama
Description: Tool for running open-source large language models locally on your own machine, with a simple command-line interface and model library.

Name: OpenAI
Description: Leading AI research lab with ChatGPT and other models.

Name: OpenRouter
Description: Unified API gateway routing to 315+ models across providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, Meta, Mistral) through one OpenAI-compatible endpoint. 50 free requests/day across 32+ free models; no .edu required.

Name: Perplexity + Comet (1-Year Bundle)
Description: Perplexity's Comet browser plus a full year of Pro access, free for students who verify with a .edu email (normally a $240/year value).

Name: Perplexity AI
Description: AI-powered search and research engine.

Name: Perplexity Pro (Education Plan)
Description: AI-powered search and research assistant with deep citations, file uploads, and access to GPT-5, Claude Sonnet, and Gemini Pro. One month of Pro free on .edu signup via SheerID verification, then $10/month.

Name: Qwen
Description: AI chat platform from Alibaba featuring the Qwen family of language models.

Name: StudentOffers — AI & ML Directory
Description: Curated directory of verified student discounts and free offers across AI and ML tools, including API credits and unified gateways. Most offers require .edu email verification.

Name: StudentOffers — Free LLM API Proxy
Description: Self-hosted OpenAI-compatible proxy aggregating free-tier API keys from 11+ providers across 60+ models — over 1 billion free tokens per month from a single endpoint. Requires setting up your own accounts with each underlying provider.

Name: xAI Grok API
Description: API access to xAI's Grok models, with $25 in free one-time API credits on signup. Students with a .edu email also get 60 days of SuperGrok premium chat access free.

Name: Z.ai
Description: AI chat platform from Zhipu AI featuring the GLM family of language models.
